concerns.text: The regression model uses both crossing number (c) and braid index (b) as independent predictors, yet the braid index is mathematically constrained by c (b ≤ c). This deterministic relationship creates severe multicollinearity, making the joint regression coefficients non‑interpretable and rendering the model descriptively redundant. The plan should either (a) treat one invariant as the sole predictor, (b) transform the predictors to an orthogonal basis, or (c) explicitly limit claims to descriptive variance partitioning with a justification that the chosen model adds analytical value beyond reporting the known inequality.

The validation of hyperbolic volume by comparing Knot Atlas values to KnotInfo assumes statistical independence between the two sources, but both databases largely derive from the same underlying knot tables. Consequently, a ≥90 % match does not constitute an independent verification of the volume measurements; it is merely a consistency check. The plan must acknowledge this limitation explicitly and avoid presenting the cross‑check as independent validation of the target variable.
